test_description: For each masked map, remove the unmasked-sky mean, harmonic-filter the temperature field to 2<=ell<=64 using the same masked-pseudo-alm procedure for data and simulations, and reconstruct the low-l map T_L(n). On unmasked pixels form P(n)=T_L(n)^2, then fit P(n)=p0+d·n by equal-weight least squares over the mask. Return the single scalar A_P=|d|/p0, the fractional dipole amplitude of the low-l bandpower, and rank the Planck SMICA value against the 1000 LCDM simulations.
test_hypothesis: The Planck SMICA map has an anomalously large fractional dipole amplitude in the squared low-ell temperature field, indicating stronger low-l bandpower asymmetry than expected under LCDM simulations.
justificaton: The statistic A_P=|d|/p0 is a nonnegative dipole-amplitude measure, and the code explicitly treats larger A_P values as more anomalous, ranking SMICA in the upper tail of the simulation distribution.

custom_summary.test_description: Low-l Bandpower Dipole Fraction: remove the unmasked-sky mean, compute masked pseudo-alm coefficients with masked pixels filled by zero, keep 2<=ell<=64, reconstruct the low-l temperature map, square it on the unmasked sky, fit P(n)=p0+d.n by equal-weight least squares, and return A_P=|d|/p0.
custom_summary.statistic: A_P = |d|/p0 from P(n)=T_L(n)^2 after a masked-pseudo-alm 2<=ell<=64 filter
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail; larger A_P means a stronger dipole in the low-l bandpower map

plot_spec.title: Low-l bandpower dipole fraction
plot_spec.caption: Distribution of the fractional dipole amplitude of the squared low-l temperature field. The SMICA value is compared with the upper tail of the simulations.

custom_summary.interpretation: SMICA is not in the highest 5% tail for this low-l bandpower dipole fraction.

test_summary.Description: Mean-subtracted masked maps were pseudo-alm filtered to 2<=ell<=64, squared on the unmasked sky, and fit as P(n)=p0+d·n; the statistic is A_P=|d|/p0.
test_summary.Results: Planck SMICA gives A_P=0.3368. The 1000 LCDM simulations have mean 0.2182 and std 0.0961; 113 simulations are larger, giving an upper-tail p=0.113 and z≈1.23.
test_summary.Interpretation: SMICA shows a higher-than-average low-ell bandpower dipole fraction, but it is not in the upper 5% tail and is not statistically anomalous under the LCDM simulations.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: This is related to the well-documented hemispherical power asymmetry/dipole-modulation and local-variance dipole literature, including Planck 2018 isotropy results, which confirm large-angle temperature anomalies while finding broad consistency with LCDM elsewhere (Planck Collaboration 2020, A&A 641, A7; arXiv:1906.02552, https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.02552). The present squared low-l bandpower-dipole statistic is not identical to those standard estimators.
